source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/CompetingRiskAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D5 LOOSE smoke oracle (self-computed from the ported C++ core, NOT yet emitter-verified; D6 tightens to exact). CompetingRiskAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a Gumbel+Weibull CompetingRisksModel and assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. Values reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python. thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Parameter layout [Gumbel location, Gumbel scale, Weibull scale, Weibull shape]; rmse over the competing-risks inverse-CDF grid can be non-finite and is NOT asserted. Tolerances loose (rel 1e-5). D6 oracle-exemption: this analysis target has NO C# numeric oracle (D1/D2 shipped the per-family analyses as STRUCTURAL oracles only; the seeded forecast trajectory is not C#-reproducible -- documented P4-class MersenneTwister-seed concern) and the emitter has no driver for it, so the fixture carries "oracle_skip": true: the dev-only dotnet gate (verify_oracles.py) SKIPS these assertions while the shipped C++/R/Python harnesses still check the self-computed values. See docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md.
